prose:
**Phenol Fukui reactivity descriptors** — Local Fukui functions (f+ for electrophilic attack, f– for nucleophilic attack, f0 and dual descriptor) computed via the chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill. Level of theory: DFT (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, tier `standard`), exact four-center integrals (density fitting OFF), PySCF backend; gas phase; charge 0; multiplicity 1. Geometry: single-point on the user-supplied `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz` (no prior optimization at this level). SCF for neutral (RKS), cation and anion (UKS) all converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true. Warnings reported (verbatim):
- "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
- "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
- "common header present"
